clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL), thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL), and anemia (9.8 g/dL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggests hepatic involvement. AKI progression from Stage 1 (2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (8.9 mg/dL) indicates acute kidney injury. Platelets remain below 100 K/uL, meeting SOFA coagulation scoring criteria.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and thrombocytopenia meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2
Creatinine rose from 2.1 to 8.9 mg/dL (excluded outlier), meeting KDIGO Stage 3 criteria
Platelets <100 K/uL for 36 hours meets SOFA coag score ≥2 criteria
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but hypotension and vasopressor use status unknown

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order fluid resuscitation and reassess perfusion status
Monitor AKI progression with hourly urine output and creatinine trends
Consider platelet transfusion if thrombocytopenia <50 K/uL persists
Evaluate for source control of infection (imaging/labs)
doctor_handoff: Review excluded creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) for specimen integrity. Confirm vasopressor requirements and source of infection. Reassess lactate clearance after initial resuscitation.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL excluded as outlier)

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
